instructions: DEMONSTRATION FIXTURE NOTE: This file uses Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale content to demonstrate the assessment engine's capabilities. It is NOT intended as a clinical or diagnostic tool and must not be used for clinical purposes. If you have concerns about your mental health or wellbeing, please speak with a qualified healthcare professional. -- Original instructions: Below is a list of statements dealing with your general feelings about yourself. Please indicate how strongly you agree or disagree with each statement.

Self-Esteem.label: Global Self-Esteem
Self-Esteem.overview: Self-esteem reflects your overall sense of personal worth and value as an individual.
Self-Esteem.highMeaning: High scorers tend to feel positively about themselves, accept their limitations, and approach challenges with confidence.
Self-Esteem.lowMeaning: Low scorers may struggle with self-doubt, be highly self-critical, or feel they lack value compared to others.

Self-Esteem.High: You hold yourself in positive regard. Maintain this by continuing to acknowledge your strengths alongside your limitations.
Self-Esteem.Moderate: You have a mixed sense of self-worth. Reflecting on specific areas of confidence versus self-doubt may help clarify where to focus.
Self-Esteem.Low: You may be experiencing significant self-criticism. Speaking with a trusted person or counsellor could be a helpful next step.

questions.text: I feel that I am a person of worth, at least on an equal plane with others.
I feel that I have a number of good qualities.
All in all, I am inclined to feel that I am a failure.
I am able to do things as well as most other people.
I feel I do not have much to be proud of.
I take a positive attitude toward myself.
On the whole, I am satisfied with myself.
I wish I could have more respect for myself.
I certainly feel useless at times.
At times I think I am no good at all.
